data.title: Intel, Cleanspark, Super Micro Computer, Upstart, Tesla: Why These 5 Stocks Are On Investors' Radars Today
data.description:
U.S. markets saw mixed results on Monday: the Dow Jones Industrial Average slipped 0.3% to 44,782, the S&P 500 edged up 0.2% to 6,047.15, and the Nasdaq climbed nearly 1% to close at 19,403.95.



These are the top stocks that gained the attention of retail traders and investors throughout the day:



Intel Corporation

data.title: US Announces $725 Million Arms Package For Ukraine
data.description: The Pentagon announced Monday the latest arms package for Ukraine totaling $725 million, the largest since a $1 billion package in April.

data.title: Health Canada Cuts $14.6M From Cannabis Program Amid Federal Austerity
data.description: Health Canada faces CA$20.5M in cannabis program cuts over 3 years, raising concerns for the legal market amid federal inaction.

data.title: Gas Prices Fall For 7th Straight Week To $3 Per Gallon, Reaching 2021 Levels
data.description: The national average price of gas has fallen for the seventh straight week to $3 per gallon, the lowest level since May 2021. The price of gas has fallen slightly, 0.6 cents from last week, and is 23.2 cents lower than a year ago.

data.title: Elon Musk $56B Pay Package Rejected By Judge Again: Tesla Analyst Says 'A Soap Opera Playing Out In Delaware'
data.description: The saga over a 2018 pay package for Elon Musk valued at $56 billion makes headlines again with a Delaware judge again rejecting the plan.
